summary: Session 3: wired route()/verify() to the copilot-cli transport profile as a fully separate code path from the untouched api-profile body. Catalog-role resolution for the generator (_resolve_copilot_generator); the verifier provenance rule (verification.pick_copilot_cli_verifier, the picker seam -- cross_role_provider_diversity enforced, fails closed to ProvenanceUnavailable, never a same-provider pairing); the hard invocation breaker (_copilot_cli_dispatch, lock-guarded, degrades gracefully to "verification unavailable" if it trips specifically on the auto-verify dispatch rather than discarding a successful generation); cost-keyed guard exclusions (evaluate_cost_guard + 4 named constants, wired as a real defensive check); honest metrics/cost_report accounting (4 additive record_call fields, a separate "Recorded copilot-cli calls (unbilled)" cost-report line, never folding $0.00 into total cost). Routed test-generation (heavily adapted, same pattern as S1/S2) produced ai_router/tests/test_copilot_routing_integration.py (38 tests) plus extensions to test_metrics.py and test_cost_report.py. Routed code-review (auto-verified VERIFIED) found and fixed 4 issues (an inverted cost-guard default, a non-atomic breaker counter, a duplicated prefer-list walk, a stray !r in an error message); one Minor finding deliberately not fixed (matches existing api-path precedent). Cross-provider session verification ran 4 rounds, each finding a genuine, distinct, reproducible defect or a legitimately debatable architecture tradeoff -- a same-provider-slip-through path via model_name vs. model_id, a process-scoped-not-session-scoped invocation breaker (adjudicated as a disclosed, deliberate scoping decision per the reviewer's own offered alternative), and a name-prefix-heuristic fallback that lacked the catalog's own dispatch-confirmation safeguard (removed entirely) -- one Round-1 Major was confirmed a false positive with cited evidence (ai_router/config.py, unmodified this session, already populates _verification_template identically for both profiles) rather than deflected without proof. Round 4 VERIFIED. Final suite: 2478 passed, 5 skipped, one pre-existing unrelated drift_guard failure (two session sets simultaneously in-progress by explicit operator direction) that predates this session throughout. No live-seat dogfood yet (Session 4's scope, by design) and no version bump/release (Session 5's scope).

reason.specifics: Routed analysis (task_type=analysis, gemini-pro) recommended the incumbent continue Session 4: it requires a live, authenticated GitHub Copilot CLI seat on the operator's own machine (so the human operator effectively co-drives this session regardless of engine), is UAT-heavy (human-in-the-loop judgment on instruction clarity), and follows directly from Sessions 1-3's implementation context -- not a net-new-implementation case where a different provider's strengths would argue for a switch.
